channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines curated from Tier 1 and Tier 2 sources

item.title: Russia Launches Unannounced Nuclear Exercise With 65,000 Troops
item.description: Russia began its largest nuclear drills in years without prior notice, mobilizing 65,000 troops, 200 missile launchers, 140 aircraft, and 8 strategic nuclear submarines across a three-day exercise. The timing coincides with Putin's Beijing visit, analysts say it aims to influence NATO decision-making.

item.title: NATO Eastern Flank Deterrence Strategy Centers on Autonomous Zone
item.description: NATO's new deterrence plan envisions an "autonomous zone" along its eastern border where only unmanned systems operate, with linked sensors and drones to detect invading Russian forces. The concept draws lessons from Ukraine and aims to reduce casualties in a potential conflict.

item.title: China Turns to Russian Oil as Gulf Supply Drops Amid Hormuz Crisis
item.description: Chinese customs data shows Russian crude shipments to China rose 11.3% year-on-year in April as Gulf imports plunged during the Strait of Hormuz crisis. However, the discount on Russian oil has shrunk significantly due to sanctions waivers boosting global demand for Russian barrels.

item.title: IRGC Warns War Will Spread Far Beyond Region If US-Israel Resume Attacks
item.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guards warned that renewed US-Israeli attacks would trigger a conflict spreading "far beyond the region." The statement comes as Trump set a deadline of several days for a deal, threatening to resume strikes if no agreement is reached.

item.title: Taiwan Unveils New Unmanned Ground Reconnaissance Vehicle
item.description: Taiwan's NCSIST revealed a new 4x4 unmanned electric reconnaissance vehicle with autonomous driving capabilities, armed with a 12.7mm remote weapon station. The platform is designed for stealth operations and can integrate with manned-unmanned teaming concepts.

item.title: Japan secures alternative oil supplies to replace Middle East crude for summer
item.description: Japanese oil refiners will be able to secure sufficient substitute crude and petroleum products to replace Middle East supplies for summer, as the Iran conflict disrupts traditional supply chains.

item.title: US asks Ukraine to ease Belarus potash restrictions amid food security concerns
item.description: The United States is urging Ukraine to relax restrictions on potash fertilizer imports from Belarus and press European allies to follow suit, Bloomberg News reported.

item.title: Drones attack industrial areas in Russia's Stavropol region
item.description: Drone attacks targeted industrial areas in Russia's Stavropol region, the regional governor said, the latest in a series of strikes on Russian infrastructure.

item.title: Taiwan's future cannot be decided by external forces, president says
item.description: Taiwan's President Tsai Ing-wen emphasized that Taiwan's future must be determined by its own people, rejecting Chinese claims over the island democracy.

item.title: Chinese supertankers exit Hormuz as Trump, Vance talk up Iran deal
item.description: Chinese supertankers are exiting the Strait of Hormuz as the US and Iran negotiate a potential peace deal, with Trump and Vance expressing optimism about diplomatic progress.

item.title: Russia shows troops moving nuclear warheads in major exercise
item.description: Russia demonstrated troops moving nuclear warheads during a major military exercise, showcasing its strategic nuclear capabilities amid heightened tensions with the West.

item.title: Four US LNG vessels sailing to China after Trump-Xi summit
item.description: Four US liquefied natural gas vessels are sailing to China following the Trump-Xi summit, signaling continued energy trade despite broader geopolitical tensions.

item.title: Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon kill 19 including children and women
item.description: Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon killed at least 19 people including children and women, officials said, in an escalation along the Israel-Lebanon border.

item.title: US Senate advances measure curbing Trump's Iran war powers
item.description: The US Senate advanced a measure to curb President Trump's war powers regarding Iran, reflecting congressional concerns about executive authority in military operations.

item.title: Russia conducts major nuclear drills amid tensions
item.description: Russia conducted major nuclear drills, demonstrating its strategic capabilities as President Putin visited China for talks with Chinese leader Xi Jinping.

item.title: US troop withdrawal will not undermine NATO defence, Europe's top commander says
item.description: Europe's top military commander said US troop withdrawals would not undermine NATO's defensive capabilities, reassuring allies about alliance commitments.

item.title: Russia warns Latvia against letting Ukraine launch drones, threatens retaliation
item.description: Russia warned Latvia against allowing Ukraine to launch drones from its territory, threatening retaliation if the Baltic state supports Ukrainian military operations.

item.title: G7 finance ministers explore responses to Iran war fallout
item.description: G7 finance ministers met to discuss coordinated responses to the economic fallout from the Iran conflict, addressing energy markets and global trade disruptions.

item.title: China's Xi gives Putin red carpet welcome with veiled jab at US
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping welcomed Russian President Vladimir Putin with full honors, making thinly veiled criticisms of US policy during the state visit.

item.title: More than 15,800 people killed in Russia's all-out war on Ukraine, UN reports
item.description: The United Nations confirmed more than 15,800 civilian deaths in Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine, documenting the human toll of the ongoing conflict.

item.title: EU strikes deal on US trade pact ahead of Trump tariff deadline
item.description: The European Union reached a trade agreement with the United States ahead of President Trump's tariff deadline, avoiding potential trade disruptions.

item.title: UK quietly issues sanctions waivers on Russian oil products
item.description: The United Kingdom has quietly issued sanctions waivers allowing continued trade in Russian oil products, raising questions about enforcement of sanctions measures.

item.title: Ukraine reportedly strikes Russian chemical plant for second time in a week
item.description: Ukrainian forces struck a Russian chemical plant for the second time in a week, targeting military-industrial infrastructure supporting Russia's war effort.

item.title: Czechia adds to EU-wide uncertainty for Ukrainian refugees
item.description: Czech policy changes added to growing uncertainty for Ukrainian refugees across the European Union as countries reassess their protection frameworks.

item.title: US not planning to send Iran's enriched uranium to Russia as part of peace deal, Vance says
item.description: Vice President JD Vance stated the US has no plans to send Iran's enriched uranium to Russia as part of any peace agreement, clarifying US position on nuclear materials.
